actions.value: An IBAN is short for 'International Bank Account Number' and is used to identify an individual account in cross-border payments. A BIC (Business Identifier Code) or SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) code is a set of 8 or 11 digits used to identify a specific bank branch for international transfers. You should be able to find this information when logging into your online banking portal and viewing your account details.

An IBAN is short for 'International Bank Account Number' and is used to identify an individual account in cross-border payments. You should be able to find this information when logging into your online banking portal and viewing your account details.
